title: NMF
key_concepts: Non-negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) enforces that both the data and the resulting factor matrices contain only non-negative values.
This creates an additive, parts-based representation (no subtraction of features), making the latent dimensions highly interpretable compared to PCA.
Each original feature is a positive sum of the latent components.
equations: Approximation: $X \approx W H$, where $X \in \mathbb{R}_{+}^{n \times d}$, and the factor matrices $W$ and $H$ must satisfy $W \ge 0, H \ge 0$.
examples: Topic modeling: A document is represented as an additive mix of topics (no 'negative' topics).
Audio spectrograms: Sound is an additive mix of frequencies.
relations: Useful when interpretability is key, contrasting with the dense, mixed positive/negative components of PCA.
